items.titleZh: 微转移印刷（MTP）作为一种有前景的可扩展方法用于硅光子学的异质集成（根特大学、imec等）
items.descriptionZh:
比利时根特大学与imec等研究机构联合发表了一篇题为《硅光子学上的微转移印刷：教程、近期进展与展望》的技术论文，系统阐述了微转移印刷技术（Micro-Transfer Printing, MTP）在硅光子异质集成领域的前景与突破。该技术通过独特的“拾取-放置”机制，将微米级薄膜器件（如激光器、探测器、调制器）从原生衬底精准转移到硅光子波导平台，实现了异质材料（如III-V族化合物半导体、铌酸锂、聚合物等）与硅基光电子线路的高密度、高通量集成，且材料利用效率远超传统倒装焊或晶圆键合方法。

**核心技术原理与创新点**  
MTP的核心在于使用弹性聚合物印章（通常为聚二甲基硅氧烷，PDMS）通过范德华力抓取预释放的微器件——这些器件通过牺牲层腐蚀与原生衬底分离，仅靠微小的“系链”结构固定。印章的粘附力可通过接触速度、压力精确调控，从而在目标位置快速释放器件（典型转移速度可达每小时数万颗芯片）。与传统方案相比，MTP无需对每个器件进行单独对准键合，且可并行转移多个器件，同时避免了晶圆级键合所需的平整度要求和对已有硅光子电路的热预算冲击。论文特别强调了“材料复用”优势：一个III-V晶圆可反复用于多次转移（通过区域留白设计），使得每平方厘米的高性能光学材料成本降低10-100倍。

**性能参数与对比数据**  
研究团队展示了多项关键指标：转移精度优于±0.5微米（满足单模波导耦合要求），器件厚度可薄至亚微米级（如200nm厚的薄膜微环谐振器），提升了热管理能力。与传统混合集成方案对比，MTP将集成密度提升至>1000个器件/cm²（倒装焊约100-200个/cm²），吞吐量达到每小时5000次转移（对比手动拾取放置的<10次/小时）。论文还量化了良率控制：通过原位光学反馈和印章表面微结构优化，单颗转移良率达99.7%以上，阵列式转移的平行良率突破98%。

**技术影响与应用场景**  
MTP的工业化成熟度被视作硅光子从研究走向大规模商用的关键催化剂。其“即插即用”特性使得数据中心光互连中的发射机/接收机模块（尤其是多波长激光器阵列）、激光雷达（LiDAR）中的光学相控阵（OPA）以及生物传感器的片上光谱系统无需定制化CMOS工艺即可实现。此外，该技术可兼容200mm/300mm标准硅晶圆产线，推动异构集成光学引擎与电子芯片的共封装（Co-Packaged Optics, CPO），有望在未来3-5年内将每比特能耗从当前10 pJ/bit降至<1 pJ/bit。论文最后指出，当前挑战在于多材料体系中的热膨胀系数失配和印章寿命优化，但通过纳米压印辅助定位和自对准设计，MTP已进入预量产验证阶段。

items.title: Pennsylvania residents complain they are being 'bulldozed' over AI data center concerns — lambast Gov. Shapiro in two-hour town hall, say he is losing support base
items.description: Pennsylvania residents blame Governor Josh Shapiro for the large number of data center projects in the state, despite his efforts to introduce policies that will balance development with the welfare of the people.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 2 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter A group of Pennsylvania residents spent a two-hour town hall lambasting Gov. Josh Shapiro over his support for data center projects, claiming residents' concerns are being ignored and the governor is losing his support base. According to Inside Climate News, 20 speakers expressed their frustration about the threats that these projects pose to the communities surrounding them. Some of the raised issues include increasing electricity prices, massive water consumption, and noise pollution, which are some of the effects that have been widely reported around the country.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowAmong them is Kelly Donia, who lives about 20 miles northeast of Philadelphia in East Whiteland Township and identifies as a registered Democrat. She’s a vocal critic of the governor, saying that he has lost her support because of his part in the data center boom. “He is losing his base,” Donia told Inside Climate. “I want him to hear this loud and freaking clear. I’m going to make it my job to make sure that man never gets elected again for any office.” Another said the issue was about "public trust and transparency," claiming that too many Americans "are finding out about these projects after decisions have been made." Jennifer Dusart of Mechanicsburg went on to say concerned residents "have been bulldozed over."Shapiro does support data center developments within the state of Pennsylvania, but says he did not give them free rein to do anything they want. “If companies want the Commonwealth’s full support — including access to tax credits and faster permitting — they must meet strict expectations around transparency, environmental protection, and community impact,” spokesperson Rosie Lapowsky told the publication in a statement. “This is about setting a higher bar for projects, not lowering it, and ensuring development happens responsibly and in a way that benefits Pennsylvanians.”Latest Videos From You may like Six AI data centers proposed for a small town of 7,000, equal to 51 Walmart Supercenters in 17 square mile area Local political revolts threaten to derail US data center projects Small Missouri town ousts city council after it approved $6 billion AI data center The state government has taken concrete steps to insulate its citizens from increasing utility costs brought about by the massive infrastructure investments needed to deliver the power that data centers demand. The state’s Public Utility Commission (PUC) has released guidance on how utility providers should deal with high-demand users like data centers. From this, PECO, the electricity provider for Philadelphia and other parts of southeast Pennsylvania, said that it put agreements in place with data center providers that completely pass the burden of upgrading their high-voltage lines and other long-distance transmission infrastructure to these large customers. This ensures that smaller users do not have to pay for these upgrades through higher electricity bills.Still, this isn’t enough for many Pennsylvanians. State Representative Jamie Walsh (R.-Luzerne County) said that one of the reasons the state has been experiencing an inflow of data center investments is because of a 2021 law that gave tax breaks to their developers. As a way to help slow down the breakneck speed of proposed data centers in the state, State Senator Katie Muth (D.-Philadelphia) said that they want to introduce a bill that will put a three-year moratorium on all projects in the state. This would be a major decision if passed into law, making Pennsylvania join the numerous other smaller jurisdictions that have already passed a temporary ban.The tension within the state is mirrored all across the country as residents and communities push back against data center projects. Some AI hyperscalers are trying to reassure people that they will take steps so that they will have limited to no effects on the environment around these sites. However, this is too little, too late for many, as the negative effects of other projects like these have already been widely reported since last year.In fact, many issues continue to surface, like this one data center in Fayette County, Georgia, which used up 29 million gallons of water in 15 months, resulting in low pressure for everyone else. We’ve also seen recent complaints about noise pollution, especially projects that were built in close proximity to homes and public infrastructure.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsShapiro's balancing act is trying to ensure that the state enjoys the huge boon that data centers could have on Pennsylvania’s balance sheet while ensuring that the interests of the people are still met. But as people become more aware of the risks that data centers pose to their way of life, many critics say that he’s not doing enough. items.titleZh: 宾夕法尼亚州居民抱怨在AI数据中心问题上被“碾压”——两小时市政厅会议上痛批夏皮罗州长，称其正失去支持基础
items.descriptionZh:
宾夕法尼亚州一批居民在最近的一场市政厅会议上，对州长乔什·夏皮罗（Josh Shapiro）支持数据中心项目的立场进行了长达两小时的激烈抨击。居民们声称，他们的担忧被忽视，而州长正在失去自己的支持基础。据《内部气候新闻》（Inside Climate News）报道，有20位发言者表达了对这些项目给周边社区带来威胁的强烈不满。他们提出的问题包括电价上涨、大量水资源消耗和噪音污染——这些影响已在全国范围内被广泛报道。

其中一位发言者是凯莉·多尼亚（Kelly Donia），她住在费城东北约20英里的东怀特兰镇（East Whiteland Township），自称是注册民主党人。她直言不讳地批评州长，称因为他在数据中心热潮中的角色，自己已经不再支持他。“他正在失去他的基本盘，”多尼亚对《内部气候新闻》表示，“我想让他明明白白地听到这一点。我会把确保他再也选不上任何公职当成我的任务。”另一位居民则指出，问题在于“公共信任和透明度”，声称太多美国人“在决策做出之后才发现这些项目”。来自梅卡尼克斯堡（Mechanicsburg）的詹妮弗·杜萨特（Jennifer Dusart）补充说，感到担忧的民众“被无情地碾压了”。

夏皮罗确实支持宾州的数据中心发展，但他表示并未给予这些公司为所欲为的自由。“如果企业想要获得州政府的全力支持——包括税收抵免和更快的审批——它们必须在透明度、环境保护和社区影响方面满足严格的要求，”其发言人罗西·拉波斯基（Rosie Lapowsky）在一份声明中表示，“这是为了提高项目的标准，而不是降低它，确保开发以负责任的方式进行，让宾夕法尼亚人受益。”

州政府已采取具体措施来保护居民免受因数据中心所需巨额基础设施投资而导致的公用事业成本上涨的影响。宾州公共事业委员会（PUC）发布了指导意见，指导公用事业提供商如何应对像数据中心这样的高需求用户。据此，为费城及宾州东南部部分地区供电的PECO公司表示，它们已与数据中心供应商达成协议，将升级高压线路及其他长距离输电基础设施的成本完全转嫁给这些大型客户。这确保了小型用户不必通过更高的电费来承担这些升级费用。

然而，这对许多宾夕法尼亚人来说仍然不够。州众议员杰米·沃尔什（Jamie Walsh，共和党-卢泽恩县）表示，该州数据中心投资涌入的原因之一是2021年的一项法律，该法律为开发者提供了税收减免。为了帮助减缓该州数据中心项目提案的迅猛势头，州参议员凯蒂·穆斯（Katie Muth，民主党-费城）表示，他们希望提出一项法案，在全州范围内对所有数据中心项目实施三年暂停。如果该法案成为法律，将是一个重大决定，使宾州加入其他许多已经通过临时禁令的较小司法管辖区之列。

该州内部的紧张局势反映了全国各地的状况——居民和社区纷纷对数据中心项目表示反对。一些AI巨头正试图安抚民众，声称将采取措施，使这些项目对环境的影响有限甚至为零。然而，对许多人来说，这已是亡羊补牢，因为类似项目的负面影响自去年以来就已广泛报道。事实上，许多问题仍在不断浮现，例如佐治亚州费耶特县（Fayette County）的一个数据中心在15个月内消耗了2900万加仑的水，导致其他用户水压过低。近期还有关于噪音污染的投诉，特别是那些建在住宅和公共基础设施附近的项目。

夏皮罗的平衡之举是试图确保该州能从数据中心带来的巨大财政红利中受益，同时仍满足民众的利益。但随着人们越来越意识到数据中心对其生活方式带来的风险，许多批评者认为他做得还不够。

items.title: Trump says China is blocking Nvidia H200 purchases despite US approval — says country 'chose not to' sanction purchases, pushing homegrown chips instead
items.description: President Donald Trump said on Friday that Beijing is refusing to let Chinese companies buy Nvidia's H200 AI chips.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 6 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter President Donald Trump has said that Beijing is refusing to let Chinese companies buy Nvidia's H200 AI chips, telling reporters aboard Air Force One after the conclusion of a two-day summit with Chinese leader Xi Jinping that China "chose not to" approve the purchases because "they want to develop their own," according to Bloomberg.The remarks came a day after a report claiming the U.S. Commerce Department had cleared roughly 10 Chinese firms to buy H200s, including Alibaba, Tencent, ByteDance, and JD.com, but no chips have shipped.The U.S. side of the export licensing framework is now largely in place, with distributors Lenovo and Foxconn having also received approval. Under terms formalized in January, each H200 must pass through U.S. territory for third-party inspection before re-export to China, and Nvidia must remit a 25% fee on every sale to the U.S. Treasury.Latest Videos From You may like U.S. Commerce Secretary says Nvidia still hasn't sold any H200 AI GPUs to China — Chinese government is blocking imports in an attempt to push domestic semiconductor industry Nvidia still hasn’t sold a single H200 to China nearly three months after getting the green light from the White House Jensen Huang snubbed by White House for President Trump’s China state visit None of that matters if Beijing won't let its companies place orders. Trump said Friday that the topic came up during his meetings with Xi and that "something could happen," but characteristically offered no specifics. U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer said the same day that any movement on H200 purchases is now up to China.Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick made a similar point last month, saying that Beijing has blocked imports in an effort to steer investment toward domestic chipmakers like Huawei. Chinese companies that had placed purchase orders with Nvidia earlier this year later informed the company they couldn’t follow through.Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang wasn’t on the original delegation list for the Beijing summit but was added at the last minute, boarding Air Force One during a refueling stop in Alaska. His presence raised expectations of a breakthrough on H200 sales, but Trump's post-summit comments suggest the impasse remains. Tom's Hardware has reached out to Nvidia for comment.Nvidia's $78 billion annual revenue guidance assumes zero H200 recovery from China, but analysts estimate a functioning export framework would restore $3.5 billion to $4 billion in annual revenue from the country, where Nvidia's market share has fallen from roughly 95% to what Huang has described as essentially zero.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsTrump also said he discussed AI guardrails with Xi during the summit, describing them as "standard guardrails that we talk about all the time." U.S. officials had previewed the topic ahead of the trip, telling reporters the administration would explore opening a dedicated bilateral channel for regular AI discussions.The White House last month also unveiled measures targeting distillation attacks, a practice in which Chinese AI developers are accused of extracting outputs from frontier U.S. models built by companies including OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google to train competing systems at lower cost. items.titleZh: 特朗普称尽管美国批准，中国仍阻止购买英伟达H200芯片——
items.descriptionZh:
美国总统特朗普在结束与中国国家主席习近平的两天峰会后，于空军一号上向记者透露，北京方面拒绝让中国企业购买英伟达的H200 AI芯片，原因是“中国希望发展自己的芯片产业”。这一表态源于此前有报道称，美国商务部已批准约10家中国公司（包括阿里巴巴、腾讯、字节跳动和京东）采购H200的许可，但至今没有任何芯片实际出货。

H200是英伟达基于Hopper架构的旗舰级AI加速器，采用HBM3e高带宽内存，相比前代H100在内存带宽和容量上实现显著提升，专门针对大规模语言模型训练和推理优化。然而，这项技术目前在中美科技博弈中成为关键棋子。美国出口许可框架已基本到位：分销商联想和富士通已获批准，且根据1月正式生效的细则，每颗H200在再出口至中国前必须经过美国本土的第三方检查，英伟达还需向美国财政部缴纳每笔销售额25%的费用。

但所有程序都无法解决根本问题——北京不允许本国企业下单。特朗普表示，他在与习近平时提及此事，并说“可能会有进展”，但未透露具体细节。美国贸易代表杰米森·格里尔同日表示，H200采购的任何进展现在取决于中国。美国商务部长霍华德·卢特尼克上月也指出，北京正通过阻止进口来引导投资流向华为等本土芯片制造商。据悉，今年早些时候已向英伟达下单的中国企业后来告知公司无法完成交易。

英伟达CEO黄仁勋最初并未列入北京峰会代表团名单，但在最后一刻加入，于空军一号在阿拉斯加加油时登机。其出现曾引发市场对H200销售突破的预期，但特朗普的会后表态显示僵局依旧。英伟达年度营收指导（780亿美元）假设中国市场零恢复，但分析师估计，若出口框架正常运作，每年可为中国市场带来35亿至40亿美元收入——在这个市场，英伟达的份额已从约95%暴跌至黄仁勋所说的“几乎为零”。

特朗普还透露，他在峰会期间与习近平讨论了AI“护栏”，描述为“我们一直在谈论的标准护栏”。美方官员此前曾预告，将探索开设美中双边AI定期对话渠道。白宫上月还发布了针对“蒸馏攻击”的措施——中国AI开发者被指控通过提取OpenAI、Anthropic和Google等美国前沿模型的输出来低成本训练竞品系统。这场围绕H200的博弈，本质上是美国试图保持技术优势与中国推动半导体自主之间的角力，而英伟达则夹在中间，成为地缘政治风暴中最重要的芯片企业之一。

items.title: Samsung starts winding down chip production six days before planned 18-day strike — company enters 'emergency management mode,' daily losses could hit $2 billion
items.description: Samsung Electronics has reportedly begun throttling semiconductor output by cutting new wafer input and placing lithography, etching, and cleaning equipment on standby.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 6 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Ahead of industrial action by disgruntled employees over bonuses, Samsung has reportedly begun throttling semiconductor output by cutting new wafer input and placing lithography, etching, and cleaning equipment on standby, according to a report from both the Korea Herald and Seoul Economic Daily.The pre-strike adjustments, described as “emergency management mode," are designed to limit losses at facilities that normally operate around the clock. However, they also mean that Samsung's chip production is already declining days before the union's 18-day walkout is scheduled to begin on May 21st.The process involves restricting how many new wafers are fed into production lines and changing the product mix to focus on higher-value chips like HBM and advanced node semiconductors, per a source reported by the Herald.Latest Videos From You may like Samsung's critical union negotiations break down eight days before planned 18-day chip factory strike that's projected to cost $700 million per day Samsung holds desperate final talks with union over 18-day chip factory strike that could cost $20 billion Union rally causes Samsung fab production to plummet by 58% during night shift as workers demand up to $400,000 bonuses This slowdown adds to a timeline of disruption that’ll extend well beyond the planned strike dates. KB Securities analyst Kim Dong-won estimated in a recent report that restarting and stabilizing Samsung's highly automated production lines after an 18-day stoppage could take an additional two to three weeks. Combined with this pre-strike wind-down period, Samsung's total window of reduced output could stretch to six weeks or longer.According to the Seoul Economic Daily, daily losses could approach 3 trillion won ($2 billion) if fabrication lines are paused entirely. Professor Kwon Seok-joon at Sungkyunkwan University previously estimated that the 18-day walkout alone would cause 10 trillion to 17 trillion won ($17 billion) in direct losses, while JPMorgan has projected total losses of up to 43 trillion won ($28 billion) when factoring in labor costs and extended production disruption.As of yesterday, May 14th, more than 43,000 workers had signed up to participate in the walkout, approaching the union's target of 50,000. "Even at the current level, more than half of the entire semiconductor (DS) division workforce is joining, and the company judges that a de facto shutdown is imminent,” a source told the Seoul Economic Daily.Samsung sent a letter to the union today following the collapse of talks earlier this week, proposing that both sides resume talks without preconditions on Saturday, but union head Choi Seung-ho rejected the overture, telling Korean media that negotiations could take place after June 7, the scheduled end date of the strike.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsTrendForce projects that the strike could disrupt 3% to 4% of global DRAM supply and 2% to 3% of NAND supply, given Samsung's production share. The longer-term risk, however, may be reputational because Samsung is ramping up the supply of HBM and high-capacity server DRAM for AI infrastructure. Even brief delivery uncertainty could push customers toward SK hynix and Micron. items.titleZh: 三星在计划举行18天罢工前6天开始缩减芯片产能——公司进入“紧急管理模式”，日损失或高达20亿美元
items.descriptionZh:
三星电子因员工奖金纠纷即将面临大规模罢工，但在此之前已提前启动“紧急管理模式”，通过削减新晶圆投入、将光刻、刻蚀和清洗设备转为待机状态等方式限制产量。据《韩国先驱报》和《首尔经济日报》报道，三星这一预罢工调整旨在减少通常24小时运转的设施在停工期间的损失，但也意味着在5月21日工会计划进行的18天罢工开始前几天，三星的芯片产出就已经开始下降。

**核心技术原理与调整措施**  
报道援引消息人士称，三星的减产措施主要包括两方面：一是限制进入生产线的晶圆新投片量，二是调整产品组合，将资源集中在高价值芯片（如HBM高带宽存储器、先进制程半导体）上。这种预生产管理本质上是一种“控量保价”策略——通过减少低价值通用芯片的产出，优先保证高利润产品的供应，从而在罢工期间维持最大限度的营收与客户关系。由于半导体制造是高度自动化的连续流程，一旦生产线降速或停机，后续重启需要经历复杂的设备校准、环境稳定和良率爬坡过程。

**性能参数、损失与时间线**  
KB证券分析师Kim Dong-won指出，即使罢工仅持续18天，重启并稳定三星高度自动化的生产线还需额外2-3周时间。加上罢工前的减产期，三星总产量下降的窗口可能长达6周甚至更久。如果产线完全停摆，每日损失接近3万亿韩元（约20亿美元）。成均馆大学教授Kwon Seok-joon此前估算，仅罢工本身就将造成10万亿至17万亿韩元（约170亿美元）的直接损失；而摩根大通在考虑劳动力成本和更长的生产中断后，预计总损失最高可达43万亿韩元（约280亿美元）。截至5月14日，已有超过4.3万名员工报名参与罢工，接近工会5万人的目标。消息人士称：“按目前水平，整个半导体（DS）部门一半以上的员工都在加入，公司判断实际停摆已迫在眉睫。”

**罢工谈判与市场影响**  
在5月14日（周二）谈判破裂后，三星于今天（5月15日或16日）致函工会，提议周六（5月18日）在不设前提条件下恢复谈判，但工会领导人Choi Seung-ho拒绝，表示谈判可在罢工结束日（6月7日）后进行。这意味着罢工几乎不可避免。

**技术影响与应用场景**  
从全球供应链看，TrendForce预测此次罢工可能扰乱全球DRAM供应量的3%-4%、NAND供应量的2%-3%。更深远的风险在于声誉——三星正大力扩产面向AI基础设施的HBM和大容量服务器DRAM，而任何短暂的交付不确定性都可能促使客户转向SK海力士和美光。HBM是当前AI训练芯片（如NVIDIA H100/B200）的核心配套存储器，三星在此领域正追赶SK海力士；罢工导致的交付延迟将使三星在争夺AI芯片订单的竞争中处于劣势，并可能长期改变客户采购格局。此外，三星的先进节点（如GAA 3nm）生产也高度依赖稳定的设备运行，长时间的产出波动将进一步影响其技术迭代和代工客户信任。

items.title: Musk's Colossus 1 AI supercomputer's inefficient mixed-architecture design couldn't be used to train Grok, so Anthropic's using it for inference instead — Musk readies unified Blackwell-only Colossus 2 for frontier training and potential IPO
items.description: Anthropic has leased xAI’s entire 220,000-GPU Colossus 1 supercluster from SpaceX to ease Claude’s growing compute bottlenecks, in a deal that may reveal far bigger ambitions around AI infrastructure, orbital data centers, and Musk’s IPO strategy.
items.content: Share Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Last week, Anthropic announced that it had struck a deal with SpaceX to lease all of the latter's Colossus 1 data center, with over 220,000 GPUs and 300 megawatts of compute capacity. The deal immediately raises questions, foremost among them: why would Musk lease one of xAI’s most aggressively hyped AI assets to a direct rival? With SpaceX's IPO just around the corner, a related strategy appears to be at play, but it also turns out that the system's mixed architecture with different types of GPUs may be a key reason Musk has decided to lease the system.Anthropic says the newly acquired capacity will primarily be used to ease long-standing usage bottlenecks across Claude’s paid ecosystem. According to the company, the additional compute will enable significantly higher Claude Code limits, the removal of peak-hour throttling for Pro and Max subscribers, and substantially increased API request limits for Claude Opus models used by developers and enterprise customers.The seemingly unlikely partnership — a complete turnaround of Musk's earlier stance on Anthropic — also reveals Anthropic is straining under the Claude ecosystem’s compute demands. The company says it needs the entire 300 MW AI supercluster just to improve the experience of using Claude.Latest Videos From You may like Musk's SpaceX has rented out access to its supercomputer's 220,000 Nvidia GPUs and 300 megawatts of AI compute power to rival Anthropic OpenAI couldn’t finance its data centers, so it took control of the hardware instead Anthropic in early talks to buy DRAM-less AI inference chips from UK startup (Image credit: ServeTheHome)Anthropic appears to have hit the compute wallThe earliest signs that Anthropic was struggling to keep up with the computing demands of its growing user base were the increasingly aggressive usage limits placed across Claude’s services. Free users frequently complained about rapidly exhausting tokens — the units Claude assigns for processing tasks. However, the restrictions extended beyond the free tier. Paid Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise users also regularly encountered message caps, peak-hour throttling, API rate limits, and strict time-based usage ceilings on Claude Code sessions, particularly during periods of heavy demand.It was clear that Anthropic was running out of inference capacity. While training an AI model is an expensive, one-time computational undertaking, serving that model to millions of users simultaneously creates a continuous, round-the-clock demand for compute that scales directly with every new user and every new query. The apparent solution is to build more data centers, which Anthropic is apparently pursuing via massive gigawatt deals with Amazon, Google, Microsoft, and Nvidia.However, modern hyperscale AI data centers can cost tens of billions of dollars and take years to build. Utilities are increasingly struggling to supply sufficient electricity for AI projects, while land, transformers, cooling infrastructure, and high-end GPUs themselves remain constrained. There is also growing sentiment against AI infrastructure from local communities. We recently reported that a U.S. senator got physical with a reporter after a confrontation on a data center issue.Anthropic's compute capacity problem was immediate and urgent, but the solution was significantly long-term. If only there were a massive AI supercluster with hundreds of megawatts of compute power just sitting there. Turns out there was: SpaceXAI’s Colossus 1. Following the deal, Colossus 1’s entire computing power now belongs to Anthropic — for now. (Image credit: Getty Images)Musk, xAI, SpaceX, and an upcoming IPO When Musk unveiled Colossus, it was framed as one of the clearest signs that xAI intended to compete seriously with OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google at the AI frontier. The Memphis-based cluster became famous for how quickly it was assembled. Tens of thousands of Nvidia GPUs were reportedly brought online in record time, eventually scaling to over 220,000 accelerators. Musk repeatedly boasted about xAI’s future compute ambitions, including plans to expand toward million-GPU-class systems through Colossus 2.So why does he seem to have wrapped the whole thing in a neat little bow and handed it over to Anthropic, xAI's rival? One possible answer is utilization. Reports suggest that Colossus 1 may have had more available capacity than Grok’s current user base required. However, according to a detailed report by Mirae Asset Securities — a major South Korean investment bank — the bigger utilization issue was architectural. Colossus 1 is a heterogeneous cluster, mixing roughly 150,000 H100s, 50,000 H200s, and 20,000 GB200s — three different generations of Nvidia silicon running under one roof. This was largely a byproduct of how fast xAI assembled the cluster, with different GPU generations coming online as supply allowed, rather than a deliberate design choice. (Image credit: ServeTheHome)For AI training, the heterogeneous configuration creates a significant efficiency problem. Distributed training requires every GPU in the cluster to complete each computational step simultaneously before the system can advance. When the faster GB200 chips complete their work first, the entire cluster waits for the slower H100s to catch up — a well-known bottleneck known as the straggler effect. At 220,000 chips, this effect is exponential. What to read next Anthropic's Claude Mythos might be the best overall AI model for cybersecurity but cheaper models can attain similar results, research shows OpenAI has effectively abandoned first-party Stargate data centers in favor of more flexible deals Oracle and OpenAI's Abilene expansion saga detailed: 600MW expansion gets scrapped, as larger 4.5GW agreement remains on track As a result of these issues, xAI's real-world GPU utilization reportedly sat at just 11% — meaning 89% of the cluster's theoretical computing power was going to waste. For context, Meta and Google typically operate at 40% or above.AI GPUs are not static assets that quietly sit on shelves, gaining value over time. They depreciate rapidly, consume enormous amounts of electricity, and require expensive maintenance and cooling infrastructure. Unused GPUs are effectively burning money.From that perspective, Anthropic may have arrived at exactly the right moment. The company had exploding demand and an urgent need for ready-made compute, while SpaceX/xAI had a gigantic, not-so-great first-generation AI cluster. For Anthropic, however, the same cluster looked quite different. The company needed compute power for Inference — running queries through an already-trained model, which does not require the tight synchronization that training workloads demand. So, what was a structural inefficiency for xAI's training workloads is a workable infrastructure for Anthropic's inference needs.Multiple reports suggest xAI is now heavily focused on Colossus 2, a far larger next-generation cluster reportedly aimed at gigawatt-scale AI infrastructure. Unlike Colossus 1's chaotic mix of chip generations, Colossus 2 is built entirely on Nvidia's Blackwell architecture — a homogeneous cluster where every GPU is identical. In a uniform cluster, every chip completes each training step at roughly the same time, allowing GPU utilization to theoretically surpass the range in which Meta and Google currently operate. xAI can also properly optimize its software stack for a single hardware generation rather than trying to serve three simultaneously. (Image credit: Nvidia)According to the Mirae Asset report, xAI has already moved its core training workloads entirely onto Colossus 2, effectively treating Colossus 1 as a retired first-generation asset. In other words, Colossus 1 may have transitioned from "cutting-edge frontier training weapon" into a monetizable first-generation compute asset, while Musk continues to build towards xAI’s “takeover” with Colossus 2.Musk has long treated his companies less like isolated entities and more like interconnected pieces of a broader ecosystem. Tesla technologies appear across SpaceX projects. SpaceX infrastructure supports xAI ambitions. xAI products increasingly feed into Musk’s wider platform strategy.The deal also hints at another possibility: Musk could be positioning SpaceX/xAI as more of an AI cloud infrastructure provider. That would not be entirely surprising. xAI has already launched Grok Business and enterprise-focused offerings featuring APIs, security controls, audit logging, and corporate integrations. This also aligns with Musk’s reported plans for broader structural changes at SpaceX and xAI ahead of the company's upcoming IPO.Earlier this year, Musk publicly attacked Anthropic and Claude, calling the company “misanthropic and evil.” Yet this week, he claimed he approved the deal after speaking with Anthropic executives and determining that “no one set off my evil detector.”Mirae Asset’s analysts attempted to estimate the value of the Anthropic deal, using estimated hourly lease rates for different Nvidia GPU types. The analysts projected that Colossus 1 could theoretically generate roughly $5–6 billion in annual revenue. That nearly perfectly offsets xAI's annualized net loss of approximately $6 billion as of Q1 2026, effectively pulling the company to breakeven in a single contract.For Anthropic, the analysts applied CEO Dario Amodei's own publicly stated estimate that roughly half of all AI industry compute spending goes toward inference, and that inference compute converts to revenue at a 3x multiplier. On that basis, the $5 billion being directed toward inference capacity could generate approximately $15 billion in incremental ARR — a significant addition to Anthropic's already rapidly growing revenue base.Stellar ambitionAnother critical aspect of the announcement involved “orbital AI compute capacity” — basically, data centers in space. Granted, it does sound like science fiction marketing language. But it directly ties into a core problem both companies, alongside several other AI giants, are increasingly facing: AI infrastructure is beginning to outgrow terrestrial constraints. So when a joint announcement comes from the world's largest AI company and the company that built the world’s largest reusable rocket system and operates thousands of active satellites in orbit, you best believe we may soon have data centers floating around in space.Despite Mirae Asset’s analysis, the factual financial details of the Colossus deal are not publicly available. However, Anthropic recently raised $30 billion in a Series G funding round, valuing the company at $380 billion. It would not be too wild a guess to say some of that cash may have gone into funding the Colossus agreement. Then again, the company said last month that its annualized revenue run rate had already surpassed $30 billion, highlighting the staggering scale at which Claude’s business is now operating.xAI built Colossus 1 fast — too fast, it turned out. The resulting mixed GPU architecture created structural training inefficiencies that made the cluster hard to justify as a long-term platform. With Colossus 2 now operational and built properly on uniform Blackwell hardware, Colossus 1 became a first-generation asset in search of a better use.Anthropic, with explosive demand and not enough compute, provided exactly that. The deal converts what was effectively a depreciating liability into roughly $6 billion in annual revenue — enough to bring xAI close to breakeven. For Anthropic, the same compute could unlock an estimated $15 billion in additional ARR. Both companies got what they needed, and Musk gets a compelling infrastructure story heading into a potential IPO.
items.author: Etiido Uko
items.titleZh: 马斯克的Colossus 1 AI超级计算机因低效的混合架构设计无法用于训练Grok，因此Anthropic转而将其用于推理——马斯克正筹备统一采用Blackwell架构的Colossus 2，用于前沿训练及潜在IPO
items.descriptionZh:
上周，Anthropic宣布与SpaceX达成协议，将租用后者旗下Colossus 1数据中心的全部算力——该中心拥有超过22万块GPU和300兆瓦的计算容量。这笔交易立即引发诸多疑问：马斯克为何要将xAI最引以为傲的AI资产之一租给直接竞争对手？随着SpaceX IPO临近，背后似乎隐藏着某种关联策略，但更关键的原因可能是该系统的异构GPU架构——混合了不同代际的芯片，这或许是马斯克决定出租该系统的核心因素。

Anthropic表示，新获取的算力将主要用于缓解Claude付费生态中长期存在的使用瓶颈。具体而言，额外算力将显著提升Claude Code的额度限制，取消Pro和Max用户在高峰时段的限流，并大幅提高面向开发者和企业客户的Claude Opus模型的API请求上限。这一看似不寻常的合作——与马斯克此前对Anthropic的公开批评态度完全相反——也揭示了Anthropic正承受着Claude生态算力需求的巨大压力。该公司表示，仅为了改善Claude的使用体验，就需要整个300兆瓦的AI超级集群。

**Anthropic似乎撞上了算力墙**  
最早显示Anthropic难以跟上用户增长算力需求的迹象，是Claude服务中日益严苛的使用限制。免费用户频繁抱怨token（Claude处理任务的计量单位）迅速耗尽。但限制并不仅限于免费层。付费的Pro、Max、Team和Enterprise用户也经常遇到消息上限、高峰时段限流、API速率限制，以及Claude Code会话严格的时间使用上限，尤其是在高需求时段。显然，Anthropic的推理算力正在告急。训练AI模型虽然昂贵但是一次性的计算投入，而为数百万用户同时服务则产生持续的全天候算力需求，且随着新用户和新查询直接扩展。明显的解决方案是建设更多数据中心——Anthropic似乎正通过与亚马逊、谷歌、微软和英伟达的吉瓦级交易来推进。然而，现代超大规模AI数据中心造价动辄数百亿美元，建设周期长达数年。公用事业公司越来越难以满足AI项目的电力需求，而土地、变压器、冷却基础设施和高端GPU本身依然紧缺。此外，来自地方社区对AI基础设施的抵触情绪也在增长。我们最近曾报道，一位美国参议员因数据中心问题与记者发生肢体冲突。Anthropic的算力问题既紧迫又亟需解决，但解决方案却需要长期投入。如果恰好有一个现成的、拥有数百兆瓦算力的巨型AI超级集群就好了。事实证明，确实有：SpaceX的Colossus 1。

**马斯克、xAI、SpaceX与即将到来的IPO**  
当马斯克推出Colossus时，它被视为xAI意图在AI前沿领域与OpenAI、Anthropic和谷歌认真竞争的最明确信号之一。这个位于孟菲斯的集群以其惊人的搭建速度而闻名。据报道，数万块英伟达GPU在创纪录的时间内上线，最终扩展到超过22万个加速器。马斯克多次夸耀xAI未来的算力雄心，包括通过Colossus 2扩展到百万GPU级系统的计划。那么，他为什么似乎将整个系统打包交给了xAI的竞争对手Anthropic？一个可能的答案是利用率。有报道称，Colossus 1的可用容量可能超过了Grok当前用户群的需求。然而，根据韩国投资银行Mirae Asset Securities的一份详细报告，更大的利用率问题是架构性的。Colossus 1是一个异构集群，混合了大约15万块H100、5万块H200和2万块GB200——三代不同的英伟达芯片共处一室。这很大程度上是xAI快速组装集群的副产品：不同代际的GPU随着供应情况分批上线，而非刻意设计。

对于AI训练而言，这种异构配置带来了显著的效率问题。分布式训练要求集群中的每一块GPU同时完成每个计算步骤，系统才能继续推进。当更快的GB200芯片率先完成工作时，整个集群必须等待较慢的H100跟上——这就是众所周知的“掉队者效应”。在22万块芯片的规模下，这种效应呈指数级放大。结果，据称xAI的实际GPU利用率仅为11%——这意味着集群理论计算能力的89%被浪费了。作为对比，Meta和谷歌的利用率通常在40%以上。AI GPU并非能安静躺在架子上增值的静态资产。它们迅速贬值，消耗大量电力，且需要昂贵的维护和冷却基础设施。未使用的GPU实际上就是在烧钱。

从这个角度看，Anthropic可能恰好在正确时机出现。该公司需求激增，急需现成的算力；而SpaceX/xAI则拥有一个庞大但不太理想的第一代AI集群。然而，对Anthropic来说，同样的集群却大不相同。该公司需要算力用于推理——运行已训练好的模型处理查询，这不需要训练工作负载所需的严格同步。因此，对xAI训练工作负载而言是结构性低效的，对Anthropic的推理需求却是可用的基础设施。

多份报告显示，xAI现在正全力聚焦Colossus 2——一个规模更大的下一代集群，据报道旨在达到吉瓦级AI基础设施水平。与Colossus 1混乱的芯片代际混合不同，Colossus 2完全基于英伟达的Blackwell架构构建——一个所有GPU完全相同的同构集群。在统一集群中，每块芯片完成每个训练步骤的时间大致相同，使GPU利用率理论上可以超越Meta和谷歌当前的水平。xAI还可以针对单一硬件世代优化软件栈，而非同时服务于三代硬件。

据Mirae Asset报告，xAI已将其核心训练工作负载完全迁移到Colossus 2上，实际上将Colossus 1视为已退役的第一代资产。换句话说，Colossus 1可能已从“前沿训练利器”转变为可货币化的第一代计算资产，而马斯克则继续通过Colossus 2推进xAI的“扩张”计划。

**交易背后的财务逻辑与战略影响**  
马斯克一直将其公司视为更广泛生态系统中相互关联的部件，而非孤立实体。特斯拉的技术出现在SpaceX项目中，SpaceX基础设施支持xAI的雄心，xAI产品日益融入马斯克的整体平台战略。这笔交易也暗示了另一种可能性：马斯克可能正在将SpaceX/xAI定位为AI云基础设施提供商。这并不完全令人意外。xAI已推出Grok Business和企业级产品，提供API、安全控制、审计日志和企业集成功能。这也与马斯克在公司即将IPO前进行更广泛结构变动的计划相符。

今年早些时候，马斯克曾公开攻击Anthropic和Claude，称其“反人类且邪恶”。但本周，他声称在与Anthropic高管交谈并确定“没人触发我的邪恶探测器”后批准了这笔交易。

Mirae Asset的分析师试图估算这笔交易的价值，他们采用不同类型英伟达GPU的估计小时租赁费率。分析师预计，Colossus 1理论上每年可产生约50-60亿美元的收入。这几乎完全抵消了xAI截至2026年第一季度约60亿美元的年化净亏损，使公司凭借单一合同实现盈亏平衡。对于Anthropic，分析师采用了CEO Dario Amodei本人公开的估计：AI行业约一半的计算支出用于推理，且推理算力转化为收入时具有3倍的乘数效应。据此，用于推理能力的50亿美元可产生约150亿美元的增量年度经常性收入——对已快速增长的收入基础来说是重大补充。

**超越地球的雄心：轨道AI计算**  
公告中另一个关键方面涉及“轨道AI计算能力”——基本上就是太空中的数据中心。诚然，这听起来像科幻营销语言。但它直接关联到这两家公司以及其他AI巨头日益面临的核心问题：AI基础设施开始超越地球限制。因此，当全球最大的AI公司与建造了全球最大可重复使用火箭系统并运营着数千颗在轨卫星的公司联合发布这样的声明时，我们最好相信太空数据中心可能很快成为现实。

尽管有Mirae Asset的分析，但Colossus交易的实际财务细节并未公开。然而，Anthropic最近在G轮融资中筹集了300亿美元，估值达3800亿美元。不难推测其中部分资金可能用于资助Colossus协议。此外，该公司上个月表示其年化收入运行率已超过300亿美元，凸显了Claude业务当前的惊人规模。

**总结**  
xAI快速建造了Colossus 1——事实证明太快了。由此产生的混合GPU架构造成了结构性的训练低效，使得该集群难以作为长期平台维持。随着Colossus 2现在基于统一的Blackwell硬件正常运行，Colossus 1成为寻找更好用途的第一代资产。需求爆发且算力不足的Anthropic恰好提供了这个机会。这笔交易将实际上正在贬值的负债转化为约60亿美元的年收入——足以使xAI接近盈亏平衡。对Anthropic而言，同样的算力可解锁预估150亿美元的额外年度经常性收入。两家公司各取所需，而马斯克在可能IPO前夕获得了一个引人注目的基础设施故事。

history.titleZh: 微转移印刷（MTP）作为一种有前景的可扩展方法用于硅光子学的异质集成（根特大学、imec等）
history.descriptionZh:
比利时根特大学与imec等研究机构联合发表了一篇题为《硅光子学上的微转移印刷：教程、近期进展与展望》的技术论文，系统阐述了微转移印刷技术（Micro-Transfer Printing, MTP）在硅光子异质集成领域的前景与突破。该技术通过独特的“拾取-放置”机制，将微米级薄膜器件（如激光器、探测器、调制器）从原生衬底精准转移到硅光子波导平台，实现了异质材料（如III-V族化合物半导体、铌酸锂、聚合物等）与硅基光电子线路的高密度、高通量集成，且材料利用效率远超传统倒装焊或晶圆键合方法。

**核心技术原理与创新点**  
MTP的核心在于使用弹性聚合物印章（通常为聚二甲基硅氧烷，PDMS）通过范德华力抓取预释放的微器件——这些器件通过牺牲层腐蚀与原生衬底分离，仅靠微小的“系链”结构固定。印章的粘附力可通过接触速度、压力精确调控，从而在目标位置快速释放器件（典型转移速度可达每小时数万颗芯片）。与传统方案相比，MTP无需对每个器件进行单独对准键合，且可并行转移多个器件，同时避免了晶圆级键合所需的平整度要求和对已有硅光子电路的热预算冲击。论文特别强调了“材料复用”优势：一个III-V晶圆可反复用于多次转移（通过区域留白设计），使得每平方厘米的高性能光学材料成本降低10-100倍。

**性能参数与对比数据**  
研究团队展示了多项关键指标：转移精度优于±0.5微米（满足单模波导耦合要求），器件厚度可薄至亚微米级（如200nm厚的薄膜微环谐振器），提升了热管理能力。与传统混合集成方案对比，MTP将集成密度提升至>1000个器件/cm²（倒装焊约100-200个/cm²），吞吐量达到每小时5000次转移（对比手动拾取放置的<10次/小时）。论文还量化了良率控制：通过原位光学反馈和印章表面微结构优化，单颗转移良率达99.7%以上，阵列式转移的平行良率突破98%。

**技术影响与应用场景**  
MTP的工业化成熟度被视作硅光子从研究走向大规模商用的关键催化剂。其“即插即用”特性使得数据中心光互连中的发射机/接收机模块（尤其是多波长激光器阵列）、激光雷达（LiDAR）中的光学相控阵（OPA）以及生物传感器的片上光谱系统无需定制化CMOS工艺即可实现。此外，该技术可兼容200mm/300mm标准硅晶圆产线，推动异构集成光学引擎与电子芯片的共封装（Co-Packaged Optics, CPO），有望在未来3-5年内将每比特能耗从当前10 pJ/bit降至<1 pJ/bit。论文最后指出，当前挑战在于多材料体系中的热膨胀系数失配和印章寿命优化，但通过纳米压印辅助定位和自对准设计，MTP已进入预量产验证阶段。

history.title: Pennsylvania residents complain they are being 'bulldozed' over AI data center concerns — lambast Gov. Shapiro in two-hour town hall, say he is losing support base
history.description: Pennsylvania residents blame Governor Josh Shapiro for the large number of data center projects in the state, despite his efforts to introduce policies that will balance development with the welfare of the people.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 2 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter A group of Pennsylvania residents spent a two-hour town hall lambasting Gov. Josh Shapiro over his support for data center projects, claiming residents' concerns are being ignored and the governor is losing his support base. According to Inside Climate News, 20 speakers expressed their frustration about the threats that these projects pose to the communities surrounding them. Some of the raised issues include increasing electricity prices, massive water consumption, and noise pollution, which are some of the effects that have been widely reported around the country.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowAmong them is Kelly Donia, who lives about 20 miles northeast of Philadelphia in East Whiteland Township and identifies as a registered Democrat. She’s a vocal critic of the governor, saying that he has lost her support because of his part in the data center boom. “He is losing his base,” Donia told Inside Climate. “I want him to hear this loud and freaking clear. I’m going to make it my job to make sure that man never gets elected again for any office.” Another said the issue was about "public trust and transparency," claiming that too many Americans "are finding out about these projects after decisions have been made." Jennifer Dusart of Mechanicsburg went on to say concerned residents "have been bulldozed over."Shapiro does support data center developments within the state of Pennsylvania, but says he did not give them free rein to do anything they want. “If companies want the Commonwealth’s full support — including access to tax credits and faster permitting — they must meet strict expectations around transparency, environmental protection, and community impact,” spokesperson Rosie Lapowsky told the publication in a statement. “This is about setting a higher bar for projects, not lowering it, and ensuring development happens responsibly and in a way that benefits Pennsylvanians.”Latest Videos From You may like Six AI data centers proposed for a small town of 7,000, equal to 51 Walmart Supercenters in 17 square mile area Local political revolts threaten to derail US data center projects Small Missouri town ousts city council after it approved $6 billion AI data center The state government has taken concrete steps to insulate its citizens from increasing utility costs brought about by the massive infrastructure investments needed to deliver the power that data centers demand. The state’s Public Utility Commission (PUC) has released guidance on how utility providers should deal with high-demand users like data centers. From this, PECO, the electricity provider for Philadelphia and other parts of southeast Pennsylvania, said that it put agreements in place with data center providers that completely pass the burden of upgrading their high-voltage lines and other long-distance transmission infrastructure to these large customers. This ensures that smaller users do not have to pay for these upgrades through higher electricity bills.Still, this isn’t enough for many Pennsylvanians. State Representative Jamie Walsh (R.-Luzerne County) said that one of the reasons the state has been experiencing an inflow of data center investments is because of a 2021 law that gave tax breaks to their developers. As a way to help slow down the breakneck speed of proposed data centers in the state, State Senator Katie Muth (D.-Philadelphia) said that they want to introduce a bill that will put a three-year moratorium on all projects in the state. This would be a major decision if passed into law, making Pennsylvania join the numerous other smaller jurisdictions that have already passed a temporary ban.The tension within the state is mirrored all across the country as residents and communities push back against data center projects. Some AI hyperscalers are trying to reassure people that they will take steps so that they will have limited to no effects on the environment around these sites. However, this is too little, too late for many, as the negative effects of other projects like these have already been widely reported since last year.In fact, many issues continue to surface, like this one data center in Fayette County, Georgia, which used up 29 million gallons of water in 15 months, resulting in low pressure for everyone else. We’ve also seen recent complaints about noise pollution, especially projects that were built in close proximity to homes and public infrastructure.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsShapiro's balancing act is trying to ensure that the state enjoys the huge boon that data centers could have on Pennsylvania’s balance sheet while ensuring that the interests of the people are still met. But as people become more aware of the risks that data centers pose to their way of life, many critics say that he’s not doing enough. history.titleZh: 宾夕法尼亚州居民抱怨在AI数据中心问题上被“碾压”——两小时市政厅会议上痛批夏皮罗州长，称其正失去支持基础
history.descriptionZh:
宾夕法尼亚州一批居民在最近的一场市政厅会议上，对州长乔什·夏皮罗（Josh Shapiro）支持数据中心项目的立场进行了长达两小时的激烈抨击。居民们声称，他们的担忧被忽视，而州长正在失去自己的支持基础。据《内部气候新闻》（Inside Climate News）报道，有20位发言者表达了对这些项目给周边社区带来威胁的强烈不满。他们提出的问题包括电价上涨、大量水资源消耗和噪音污染——这些影响已在全国范围内被广泛报道。

其中一位发言者是凯莉·多尼亚（Kelly Donia），她住在费城东北约20英里的东怀特兰镇（East Whiteland Township），自称是注册民主党人。她直言不讳地批评州长，称因为他在数据中心热潮中的角色，自己已经不再支持他。“他正在失去他的基本盘，”多尼亚对《内部气候新闻》表示，“我想让他明明白白地听到这一点。我会把确保他再也选不上任何公职当成我的任务。”另一位居民则指出，问题在于“公共信任和透明度”，声称太多美国人“在决策做出之后才发现这些项目”。来自梅卡尼克斯堡（Mechanicsburg）的詹妮弗·杜萨特（Jennifer Dusart）补充说，感到担忧的民众“被无情地碾压了”。

夏皮罗确实支持宾州的数据中心发展，但他表示并未给予这些公司为所欲为的自由。“如果企业想要获得州政府的全力支持——包括税收抵免和更快的审批——它们必须在透明度、环境保护和社区影响方面满足严格的要求，”其发言人罗西·拉波斯基（Rosie Lapowsky）在一份声明中表示，“这是为了提高项目的标准，而不是降低它，确保开发以负责任的方式进行，让宾夕法尼亚人受益。”

州政府已采取具体措施来保护居民免受因数据中心所需巨额基础设施投资而导致的公用事业成本上涨的影响。宾州公共事业委员会（PUC）发布了指导意见，指导公用事业提供商如何应对像数据中心这样的高需求用户。据此，为费城及宾州东南部部分地区供电的PECO公司表示，它们已与数据中心供应商达成协议，将升级高压线路及其他长距离输电基础设施的成本完全转嫁给这些大型客户。这确保了小型用户不必通过更高的电费来承担这些升级费用。

然而，这对许多宾夕法尼亚人来说仍然不够。州众议员杰米·沃尔什（Jamie Walsh，共和党-卢泽恩县）表示，该州数据中心投资涌入的原因之一是2021年的一项法律，该法律为开发者提供了税收减免。为了帮助减缓该州数据中心项目提案的迅猛势头，州参议员凯蒂·穆斯（Katie Muth，民主党-费城）表示，他们希望提出一项法案，在全州范围内对所有数据中心项目实施三年暂停。如果该法案成为法律，将是一个重大决定，使宾州加入其他许多已经通过临时禁令的较小司法管辖区之列。

该州内部的紧张局势反映了全国各地的状况——居民和社区纷纷对数据中心项目表示反对。一些AI巨头正试图安抚民众，声称将采取措施，使这些项目对环境的影响有限甚至为零。然而，对许多人来说，这已是亡羊补牢，因为类似项目的负面影响自去年以来就已广泛报道。事实上，许多问题仍在不断浮现，例如佐治亚州费耶特县（Fayette County）的一个数据中心在15个月内消耗了2900万加仑的水，导致其他用户水压过低。近期还有关于噪音污染的投诉，特别是那些建在住宅和公共基础设施附近的项目。

夏皮罗的平衡之举是试图确保该州能从数据中心带来的巨大财政红利中受益，同时仍满足民众的利益。但随着人们越来越意识到数据中心对其生活方式带来的风险，许多批评者认为他做得还不够。

history.title: Trump says China is blocking Nvidia H200 purchases despite US approval — says country 'chose not to' sanction purchases, pushing homegrown chips instead
history.description: President Donald Trump said on Friday that Beijing is refusing to let Chinese companies buy Nvidia's H200 AI chips.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 6 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter President Donald Trump has said that Beijing is refusing to let Chinese companies buy Nvidia's H200 AI chips, telling reporters aboard Air Force One after the conclusion of a two-day summit with Chinese leader Xi Jinping that China "chose not to" approve the purchases because "they want to develop their own," according to Bloomberg.The remarks came a day after a report claiming the U.S. Commerce Department had cleared roughly 10 Chinese firms to buy H200s, including Alibaba, Tencent, ByteDance, and JD.com, but no chips have shipped.The U.S. side of the export licensing framework is now largely in place, with distributors Lenovo and Foxconn having also received approval. Under terms formalized in January, each H200 must pass through U.S. territory for third-party inspection before re-export to China, and Nvidia must remit a 25% fee on every sale to the U.S. Treasury.Latest Videos From You may like U.S. Commerce Secretary says Nvidia still hasn't sold any H200 AI GPUs to China — Chinese government is blocking imports in an attempt to push domestic semiconductor industry Nvidia still hasn’t sold a single H200 to China nearly three months after getting the green light from the White House Jensen Huang snubbed by White House for President Trump’s China state visit None of that matters if Beijing won't let its companies place orders. Trump said Friday that the topic came up during his meetings with Xi and that "something could happen," but characteristically offered no specifics. U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer said the same day that any movement on H200 purchases is now up to China.Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick made a similar point last month, saying that Beijing has blocked imports in an effort to steer investment toward domestic chipmakers like Huawei. Chinese companies that had placed purchase orders with Nvidia earlier this year later informed the company they couldn’t follow through.Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang wasn’t on the original delegation list for the Beijing summit but was added at the last minute, boarding Air Force One during a refueling stop in Alaska. His presence raised expectations of a breakthrough on H200 sales, but Trump's post-summit comments suggest the impasse remains. Tom's Hardware has reached out to Nvidia for comment.Nvidia's $78 billion annual revenue guidance assumes zero H200 recovery from China, but analysts estimate a functioning export framework would restore $3.5 billion to $4 billion in annual revenue from the country, where Nvidia's market share has fallen from roughly 95% to what Huang has described as essentially zero.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsTrump also said he discussed AI guardrails with Xi during the summit, describing them as "standard guardrails that we talk about all the time." U.S. officials had previewed the topic ahead of the trip, telling reporters the administration would explore opening a dedicated bilateral channel for regular AI discussions.The White House last month also unveiled measures targeting distillation attacks, a practice in which Chinese AI developers are accused of extracting outputs from frontier U.S. models built by companies including OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google to train competing systems at lower cost. history.titleZh: 特朗普称尽管美国批准，中国仍阻止购买英伟达H200芯片——
history.descriptionZh:
美国总统特朗普在结束与中国国家主席习近平的两天峰会后，于空军一号上向记者透露，北京方面拒绝让中国企业购买英伟达的H200 AI芯片，原因是“中国希望发展自己的芯片产业”。这一表态源于此前有报道称，美国商务部已批准约10家中国公司（包括阿里巴巴、腾讯、字节跳动和京东）采购H200的许可，但至今没有任何芯片实际出货。

H200是英伟达基于Hopper架构的旗舰级AI加速器，采用HBM3e高带宽内存，相比前代H100在内存带宽和容量上实现显著提升，专门针对大规模语言模型训练和推理优化。然而，这项技术目前在中美科技博弈中成为关键棋子。美国出口许可框架已基本到位：分销商联想和富士通已获批准，且根据1月正式生效的细则，每颗H200在再出口至中国前必须经过美国本土的第三方检查，英伟达还需向美国财政部缴纳每笔销售额25%的费用。

但所有程序都无法解决根本问题——北京不允许本国企业下单。特朗普表示，他在与习近平时提及此事，并说“可能会有进展”，但未透露具体细节。美国贸易代表杰米森·格里尔同日表示，H200采购的任何进展现在取决于中国。美国商务部长霍华德·卢特尼克上月也指出，北京正通过阻止进口来引导投资流向华为等本土芯片制造商。据悉，今年早些时候已向英伟达下单的中国企业后来告知公司无法完成交易。

英伟达CEO黄仁勋最初并未列入北京峰会代表团名单，但在最后一刻加入，于空军一号在阿拉斯加加油时登机。其出现曾引发市场对H200销售突破的预期，但特朗普的会后表态显示僵局依旧。英伟达年度营收指导（780亿美元）假设中国市场零恢复，但分析师估计，若出口框架正常运作，每年可为中国市场带来35亿至40亿美元收入——在这个市场，英伟达的份额已从约95%暴跌至黄仁勋所说的“几乎为零”。

特朗普还透露，他在峰会期间与习近平讨论了AI“护栏”，描述为“我们一直在谈论的标准护栏”。美方官员此前曾预告，将探索开设美中双边AI定期对话渠道。白宫上月还发布了针对“蒸馏攻击”的措施——中国AI开发者被指控通过提取OpenAI、Anthropic和Google等美国前沿模型的输出来低成本训练竞品系统。这场围绕H200的博弈，本质上是美国试图保持技术优势与中国推动半导体自主之间的角力，而英伟达则夹在中间，成为地缘政治风暴中最重要的芯片企业之一。

history.title: Samsung starts winding down chip production six days before planned 18-day strike — company enters 'emergency management mode,' daily losses could hit $2 billion
history.description: Samsung Electronics has reportedly begun throttling semiconductor output by cutting new wafer input and placing lithography, etching, and cleaning equipment on standby.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 6 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Ahead of industrial action by disgruntled employees over bonuses, Samsung has reportedly begun throttling semiconductor output by cutting new wafer input and placing lithography, etching, and cleaning equipment on standby, according to a report from both the Korea Herald and Seoul Economic Daily.The pre-strike adjustments, described as “emergency management mode," are designed to limit losses at facilities that normally operate around the clock. However, they also mean that Samsung's chip production is already declining days before the union's 18-day walkout is scheduled to begin on May 21st.The process involves restricting how many new wafers are fed into production lines and changing the product mix to focus on higher-value chips like HBM and advanced node semiconductors, per a source reported by the Herald.Latest Videos From You may like Samsung's critical union negotiations break down eight days before planned 18-day chip factory strike that's projected to cost $700 million per day Samsung holds desperate final talks with union over 18-day chip factory strike that could cost $20 billion Union rally causes Samsung fab production to plummet by 58% during night shift as workers demand up to $400,000 bonuses This slowdown adds to a timeline of disruption that’ll extend well beyond the planned strike dates. KB Securities analyst Kim Dong-won estimated in a recent report that restarting and stabilizing Samsung's highly automated production lines after an 18-day stoppage could take an additional two to three weeks. Combined with this pre-strike wind-down period, Samsung's total window of reduced output could stretch to six weeks or longer.According to the Seoul Economic Daily, daily losses could approach 3 trillion won ($2 billion) if fabrication lines are paused entirely. Professor Kwon Seok-joon at Sungkyunkwan University previously estimated that the 18-day walkout alone would cause 10 trillion to 17 trillion won ($17 billion) in direct losses, while JPMorgan has projected total losses of up to 43 trillion won ($28 billion) when factoring in labor costs and extended production disruption.As of yesterday, May 14th, more than 43,000 workers had signed up to participate in the walkout, approaching the union's target of 50,000. "Even at the current level, more than half of the entire semiconductor (DS) division workforce is joining, and the company judges that a de facto shutdown is imminent,” a source told the Seoul Economic Daily.Samsung sent a letter to the union today following the collapse of talks earlier this week, proposing that both sides resume talks without preconditions on Saturday, but union head Choi Seung-ho rejected the overture, telling Korean media that negotiations could take place after June 7, the scheduled end date of the strike.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsTrendForce projects that the strike could disrupt 3% to 4% of global DRAM supply and 2% to 3% of NAND supply, given Samsung's production share. The longer-term risk, however, may be reputational because Samsung is ramping up the supply of HBM and high-capacity server DRAM for AI infrastructure. Even brief delivery uncertainty could push customers toward SK hynix and Micron. Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Luke James
history.titleZh: 三星在计划举行18天罢工前6天开始缩减芯片产能——公司进入“紧急管理模式”，日损失或高达20亿美元
history.descriptionZh:
三星电子因员工奖金纠纷即将面临大规模罢工，但在此之前已提前启动“紧急管理模式”，通过削减新晶圆投入、将光刻、刻蚀和清洗设备转为待机状态等方式限制产量。据《韩国先驱报》和《首尔经济日报》报道，三星这一预罢工调整旨在减少通常24小时运转的设施在停工期间的损失，但也意味着在5月21日工会计划进行的18天罢工开始前几天，三星的芯片产出就已经开始下降。

**核心技术原理与调整措施**  
报道援引消息人士称，三星的减产措施主要包括两方面：一是限制进入生产线的晶圆新投片量，二是调整产品组合，将资源集中在高价值芯片（如HBM高带宽存储器、先进制程半导体）上。这种预生产管理本质上是一种“控量保价”策略——通过减少低价值通用芯片的产出，优先保证高利润产品的供应，从而在罢工期间维持最大限度的营收与客户关系。由于半导体制造是高度自动化的连续流程，一旦生产线降速或停机，后续重启需要经历复杂的设备校准、环境稳定和良率爬坡过程。

**性能参数、损失与时间线**  
KB证券分析师Kim Dong-won指出，即使罢工仅持续18天，重启并稳定三星高度自动化的生产线还需额外2-3周时间。加上罢工前的减产期，三星总产量下降的窗口可能长达6周甚至更久。如果产线完全停摆，每日损失接近3万亿韩元（约20亿美元）。成均馆大学教授Kwon Seok-joon此前估算，仅罢工本身就将造成10万亿至17万亿韩元（约170亿美元）的直接损失；而摩根大通在考虑劳动力成本和更长的生产中断后，预计总损失最高可达43万亿韩元（约280亿美元）。截至5月14日，已有超过4.3万名员工报名参与罢工，接近工会5万人的目标。消息人士称：“按目前水平，整个半导体（DS）部门一半以上的员工都在加入，公司判断实际停摆已迫在眉睫。”

**罢工谈判与市场影响**  
在5月14日（周二）谈判破裂后，三星于今天（5月15日或16日）致函工会，提议周六（5月18日）在不设前提条件下恢复谈判，但工会领导人Choi Seung-ho拒绝，表示谈判可在罢工结束日（6月7日）后进行。这意味着罢工几乎不可避免。

**技术影响与应用场景**  
从全球供应链看，TrendForce预测此次罢工可能扰乱全球DRAM供应量的3%-4%、NAND供应量的2%-3%。更深远的风险在于声誉——三星正大力扩产面向AI基础设施的HBM和大容量服务器DRAM，而任何短暂的交付不确定性都可能促使客户转向SK海力士和美光。HBM是当前AI训练芯片（如NVIDIA H100/B200）的核心配套存储器，三星在此领域正追赶SK海力士；罢工导致的交付延迟将使三星在争夺AI芯片订单的竞争中处于劣势，并可能长期改变客户采购格局。此外，三星的先进节点（如GAA 3nm）生产也高度依赖稳定的设备运行，长时间的产出波动将进一步影响其技术迭代和代工客户信任。

history.title: Musk's Colossus 1 AI supercomputer's inefficient mixed-architecture design couldn't be used to train Grok, so Anthropic's using it for inference instead — Musk readies unified Blackwell-only Colossus 2 for frontier training and potential IPO
history.description: Anthropic has leased xAI’s entire 220,000-GPU Colossus 1 supercluster from SpaceX to ease Claude’s growing compute bottlenecks, in a deal that may reveal far bigger ambitions around AI infrastructure, orbital data centers, and Musk’s IPO strategy.
history.content: Share Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Last week, Anthropic announced that it had struck a deal with SpaceX to lease all of the latter's Colossus 1 data center, with over 220,000 GPUs and 300 megawatts of compute capacity. The deal immediately raises questions, foremost among them: why would Musk lease one of xAI’s most aggressively hyped AI assets to a direct rival? With SpaceX's IPO just around the corner, a related strategy appears to be at play, but it also turns out that the system's mixed architecture with different types of GPUs may be a key reason Musk has decided to lease the system.Anthropic says the newly acquired capacity will primarily be used to ease long-standing usage bottlenecks across Claude’s paid ecosystem. According to the company, the additional compute will enable significantly higher Claude Code limits, the removal of peak-hour throttling for Pro and Max subscribers, and substantially increased API request limits for Claude Opus models used by developers and enterprise customers.The seemingly unlikely partnership — a complete turnaround of Musk's earlier stance on Anthropic — also reveals Anthropic is straining under the Claude ecosystem’s compute demands. The company says it needs the entire 300 MW AI supercluster just to improve the experience of using Claude.Latest Videos From You may like Musk's SpaceX has rented out access to its supercomputer's 220,000 Nvidia GPUs and 300 megawatts of AI compute power to rival Anthropic OpenAI couldn’t finance its data centers, so it took control of the hardware instead Anthropic in early talks to buy DRAM-less AI inference chips from UK startup (Image credit: ServeTheHome)Anthropic appears to have hit the compute wallThe earliest signs that Anthropic was struggling to keep up with the computing demands of its growing user base were the increasingly aggressive usage limits placed across Claude’s services. Free users frequently complained about rapidly exhausting tokens — the units Claude assigns for processing tasks. However, the restrictions extended beyond the free tier. Paid Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise users also regularly encountered message caps, peak-hour throttling, API rate limits, and strict time-based usage ceilings on Claude Code sessions, particularly during periods of heavy demand.It was clear that Anthropic was running out of inference capacity. While training an AI model is an expensive, one-time computational undertaking, serving that model to millions of users simultaneously creates a continuous, round-the-clock demand for compute that scales directly with every new user and every new query. The apparent solution is to build more data centers, which Anthropic is apparently pursuing via massive gigawatt deals with Amazon, Google, Microsoft, and Nvidia.However, modern hyperscale AI data centers can cost tens of billions of dollars and take years to build. Utilities are increasingly struggling to supply sufficient electricity for AI projects, while land, transformers, cooling infrastructure, and high-end GPUs themselves remain constrained. There is also growing sentiment against AI infrastructure from local communities. We recently reported that a U.S. senator got physical with a reporter after a confrontation on a data center issue.Anthropic's compute capacity problem was immediate and urgent, but the solution was significantly long-term. If only there were a massive AI supercluster with hundreds of megawatts of compute power just sitting there. Turns out there was: SpaceXAI’s Colossus 1. Following the deal, Colossus 1’s entire computing power now belongs to Anthropic — for now. (Image credit: Getty Images)Musk, xAI, SpaceX, and an upcoming IPO When Musk unveiled Colossus, it was framed as one of the clearest signs that xAI intended to compete seriously with OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google at the AI frontier. The Memphis-based cluster became famous for how quickly it was assembled. Tens of thousands of Nvidia GPUs were reportedly brought online in record time, eventually scaling to over 220,000 accelerators. Musk repeatedly boasted about xAI’s future compute ambitions, including plans to expand toward million-GPU-class systems through Colossus 2.So why does he seem to have wrapped the whole thing in a neat little bow and handed it over to Anthropic, xAI's rival? One possible answer is utilization. Reports suggest that Colossus 1 may have had more available capacity than Grok’s current user base required. However, according to a detailed report by Mirae Asset Securities — a major South Korean investment bank — the bigger utilization issue was architectural. Colossus 1 is a heterogeneous cluster, mixing roughly 150,000 H100s, 50,000 H200s, and 20,000 GB200s — three different generations of Nvidia silicon running under one roof. This was largely a byproduct of how fast xAI assembled the cluster, with different GPU generations coming online as supply allowed, rather than a deliberate design choice. (Image credit: ServeTheHome)For AI training, the heterogeneous configuration creates a significant efficiency problem. Distributed training requires every GPU in the cluster to complete each computational step simultaneously before the system can advance. When the faster GB200 chips complete their work first, the entire cluster waits for the slower H100s to catch up — a well-known bottleneck known as the straggler effect. At 220,000 chips, this effect is exponential. What to read next Anthropic's Claude Mythos might be the best overall AI model for cybersecurity but cheaper models can attain similar results, research shows OpenAI has effectively abandoned first-party Stargate data centers in favor of more flexible deals Oracle and OpenAI's Abilene expansion saga detailed: 600MW expansion gets scrapped, as larger 4.5GW agreement remains on track As a result of these issues, xAI's real-world GPU utilization reportedly sat at just 11% — meaning 89% of the cluster's theoretical computing power was going to waste. For context, Meta and Google typically operate at 40% or above.AI GPUs are not static assets that quietly sit on shelves, gaining value over time. They depreciate rapidly, consume enormous amounts of electricity, and require expensive maintenance and cooling infrastructure. Unused GPUs are effectively burning money.From that perspective, Anthropic may have arrived at exactly the right moment. The company had exploding demand and an urgent need for ready-made compute, while SpaceX/xAI had a gigantic, not-so-great first-generation AI cluster. For Anthropic, however, the same cluster looked quite different. The company needed compute power for Inference — running queries through an already-trained model, which does not require the tight synchronization that training workloads demand. So, what was a structural inefficiency for xAI's training workloads is a workable infrastructure for Anthropic's inference needs.Multiple reports suggest xAI is now heavily focused on Colossus 2, a far larger next-generation cluster reportedly aimed at gigawatt-scale AI infrastructure. Unlike Colossus 1's chaotic mix of chip generations, Colossus 2 is built entirely on Nvidia's Blackwell architecture — a homogeneous cluster where every GPU is identical. In a uniform cluster, every chip completes each training step at roughly the same time, allowing GPU utilization to theoretically surpass the range in which Meta and Google currently operate. xAI can also properly optimize its software stack for a single hardware generation rather than trying to serve three simultaneously. (Image credit: Nvidia)According to the Mirae Asset report, xAI has already moved its core training workloads entirely onto Colossus 2, effectively treating Colossus 1 as a retired first-generation asset. In other words, Colossus 1 may have transitioned from "cutting-edge frontier training weapon" into a monetizable first-generation compute asset, while Musk continues to build towards xAI’s “takeover” with Colossus 2.Musk has long treated his companies less like isolated entities and more like interconnected pieces of a broader ecosystem. Tesla technologies appear across SpaceX projects. SpaceX infrastructure supports xAI ambitions. xAI products increasingly feed into Musk’s wider platform strategy.The deal also hints at another possibility: Musk could be positioning SpaceX/xAI as more of an AI cloud infrastructure provider. That would not be entirely surprising. xAI has already launched Grok Business and enterprise-focused offerings featuring APIs, security controls, audit logging, and corporate integrations. This also aligns with Musk’s reported plans for broader structural changes at SpaceX and xAI ahead of the company's upcoming IPO.Earlier this year, Musk publicly attacked Anthropic and Claude, calling the company “misanthropic and evil.” Yet this week, he claimed he approved the deal after speaking with Anthropic executives and determining that “no one set off my evil detector.”Mirae Asset’s analysts attempted to estimate the value of the Anthropic deal, using estimated hourly lease rates for different Nvidia GPU types. The analysts projected that Colossus 1 could theoretically generate roughly $5–6 billion in annual revenue. That nearly perfectly offsets xAI's annualized net loss of approximately $6 billion as of Q1 2026, effectively pulling the company to breakeven in a single contract.For Anthropic, the analysts applied CEO Dario Amodei's own publicly stated estimate that roughly half of all AI industry compute spending goes toward inference, and that inference compute converts to revenue at a 3x multiplier. On that basis, the $5 billion being directed toward inference capacity could generate approximately $15 billion in incremental ARR — a significant addition to Anthropic's already rapidly growing revenue base.Stellar ambitionAnother critical aspect of the announcement involved “orbital AI compute capacity” — basically, data centers in space. Granted, it does sound like science fiction marketing language. But it directly ties into a core problem both companies, alongside several other AI giants, are increasingly facing: AI infrastructure is beginning to outgrow terrestrial constraints. So when a joint announcement comes from the world's largest AI company and the company that built the world’s largest reusable rocket system and operates thousands of active satellites in orbit, you best believe we may soon have data centers floating around in space.Despite Mirae Asset’s analysis, the factual financial details of the Colossus deal are not publicly available. However, Anthropic recently raised $30 billion in a Series G funding round, valuing the company at $380 billion. It would not be too wild a guess to say some of that cash may have gone into funding the Colossus agreement. Then again, the company said last month that its annualized revenue run rate had already surpassed $30 billion, highlighting the staggering scale at which Claude’s business is now operating.xAI built Colossus 1 fast — too fast, it turned out. The resulting mixed GPU architecture created structural training inefficiencies that made the cluster hard to justify as a long-term platform. With Colossus 2 now operational and built properly on uniform Blackwell hardware, Colossus 1 became a first-generation asset in search of a better use.Anthropic, with explosive demand and not enough compute, provided exactly that. The deal converts what was effectively a depreciating liability into roughly $6 billion in annual revenue — enough to bring xAI close to breakeven. For Anthropic, the same compute could unlock an estimated $15 billion in additional ARR. Both companies got what they needed, and Musk gets a compelling infrastructure story heading into a potential IPO.
history.author: Etiido Uko
history.titleZh: 马斯克的Colossus 1 AI超级计算机因低效的混合架构设计无法用于训练Grok，因此Anthropic转而将其用于推理——马斯克正筹备统一采用Blackwell架构的Colossus 2，用于前沿训练及潜在IPO
history.descriptionZh:
上周，Anthropic宣布与SpaceX达成协议，将租用后者旗下Colossus 1数据中心的全部算力——该中心拥有超过22万块GPU和300兆瓦的计算容量。这笔交易立即引发诸多疑问：马斯克为何要将xAI最引以为傲的AI资产之一租给直接竞争对手？随着SpaceX IPO临近，背后似乎隐藏着某种关联策略，但更关键的原因可能是该系统的异构GPU架构——混合了不同代际的芯片，这或许是马斯克决定出租该系统的核心因素。

Anthropic表示，新获取的算力将主要用于缓解Claude付费生态中长期存在的使用瓶颈。具体而言，额外算力将显著提升Claude Code的额度限制，取消Pro和Max用户在高峰时段的限流，并大幅提高面向开发者和企业客户的Claude Opus模型的API请求上限。这一看似不寻常的合作——与马斯克此前对Anthropic的公开批评态度完全相反——也揭示了Anthropic正承受着Claude生态算力需求的巨大压力。该公司表示，仅为了改善Claude的使用体验，就需要整个300兆瓦的AI超级集群。

**Anthropic似乎撞上了算力墙**  
最早显示Anthropic难以跟上用户增长算力需求的迹象，是Claude服务中日益严苛的使用限制。免费用户频繁抱怨token（Claude处理任务的计量单位）迅速耗尽。但限制并不仅限于免费层。付费的Pro、Max、Team和Enterprise用户也经常遇到消息上限、高峰时段限流、API速率限制，以及Claude Code会话严格的时间使用上限，尤其是在高需求时段。显然，Anthropic的推理算力正在告急。训练AI模型虽然昂贵但是一次性的计算投入，而为数百万用户同时服务则产生持续的全天候算力需求，且随着新用户和新查询直接扩展。明显的解决方案是建设更多数据中心——Anthropic似乎正通过与亚马逊、谷歌、微软和英伟达的吉瓦级交易来推进。然而，现代超大规模AI数据中心造价动辄数百亿美元，建设周期长达数年。公用事业公司越来越难以满足AI项目的电力需求，而土地、变压器、冷却基础设施和高端GPU本身依然紧缺。此外，来自地方社区对AI基础设施的抵触情绪也在增长。我们最近曾报道，一位美国参议员因数据中心问题与记者发生肢体冲突。Anthropic的算力问题既紧迫又亟需解决，但解决方案却需要长期投入。如果恰好有一个现成的、拥有数百兆瓦算力的巨型AI超级集群就好了。事实证明，确实有：SpaceX的Colossus 1。

**马斯克、xAI、SpaceX与即将到来的IPO**  
当马斯克推出Colossus时，它被视为xAI意图在AI前沿领域与OpenAI、Anthropic和谷歌认真竞争的最明确信号之一。这个位于孟菲斯的集群以其惊人的搭建速度而闻名。据报道，数万块英伟达GPU在创纪录的时间内上线，最终扩展到超过22万个加速器。马斯克多次夸耀xAI未来的算力雄心，包括通过Colossus 2扩展到百万GPU级系统的计划。那么，他为什么似乎将整个系统打包交给了xAI的竞争对手Anthropic？一个可能的答案是利用率。有报道称，Colossus 1的可用容量可能超过了Grok当前用户群的需求。然而，根据韩国投资银行Mirae Asset Securities的一份详细报告，更大的利用率问题是架构性的。Colossus 1是一个异构集群，混合了大约15万块H100、5万块H200和2万块GB200——三代不同的英伟达芯片共处一室。这很大程度上是xAI快速组装集群的副产品：不同代际的GPU随着供应情况分批上线，而非刻意设计。

对于AI训练而言，这种异构配置带来了显著的效率问题。分布式训练要求集群中的每一块GPU同时完成每个计算步骤，系统才能继续推进。当更快的GB200芯片率先完成工作时，整个集群必须等待较慢的H100跟上——这就是众所周知的“掉队者效应”。在22万块芯片的规模下，这种效应呈指数级放大。结果，据称xAI的实际GPU利用率仅为11%——这意味着集群理论计算能力的89%被浪费了。作为对比，Meta和谷歌的利用率通常在40%以上。AI GPU并非能安静躺在架子上增值的静态资产。它们迅速贬值，消耗大量电力，且需要昂贵的维护和冷却基础设施。未使用的GPU实际上就是在烧钱。

从这个角度看，Anthropic可能恰好在正确时机出现。该公司需求激增，急需现成的算力；而SpaceX/xAI则拥有一个庞大但不太理想的第一代AI集群。然而，对Anthropic来说，同样的集群却大不相同。该公司需要算力用于推理——运行已训练好的模型处理查询，这不需要训练工作负载所需的严格同步。因此，对xAI训练工作负载而言是结构性低效的，对Anthropic的推理需求却是可用的基础设施。

多份报告显示，xAI现在正全力聚焦Colossus 2——一个规模更大的下一代集群，据报道旨在达到吉瓦级AI基础设施水平。与Colossus 1混乱的芯片代际混合不同，Colossus 2完全基于英伟达的Blackwell架构构建——一个所有GPU完全相同的同构集群。在统一集群中，每块芯片完成每个训练步骤的时间大致相同，使GPU利用率理论上可以超越Meta和谷歌当前的水平。xAI还可以针对单一硬件世代优化软件栈，而非同时服务于三代硬件。

据Mirae Asset报告，xAI已将其核心训练工作负载完全迁移到Colossus 2上，实际上将Colossus 1视为已退役的第一代资产。换句话说，Colossus 1可能已从“前沿训练利器”转变为可货币化的第一代计算资产，而马斯克则继续通过Colossus 2推进xAI的“扩张”计划。

**交易背后的财务逻辑与战略影响**  
马斯克一直将其公司视为更广泛生态系统中相互关联的部件，而非孤立实体。特斯拉的技术出现在SpaceX项目中，SpaceX基础设施支持xAI的雄心，xAI产品日益融入马斯克的整体平台战略。这笔交易也暗示了另一种可能性：马斯克可能正在将SpaceX/xAI定位为AI云基础设施提供商。这并不完全令人意外。xAI已推出Grok Business和企业级产品，提供API、安全控制、审计日志和企业集成功能。这也与马斯克在公司即将IPO前进行更广泛结构变动的计划相符。

今年早些时候，马斯克曾公开攻击Anthropic和Claude，称其“反人类且邪恶”。但本周，他声称在与Anthropic高管交谈并确定“没人触发我的邪恶探测器”后批准了这笔交易。

Mirae Asset的分析师试图估算这笔交易的价值，他们采用不同类型英伟达GPU的估计小时租赁费率。分析师预计，Colossus 1理论上每年可产生约50-60亿美元的收入。这几乎完全抵消了xAI截至2026年第一季度约60亿美元的年化净亏损，使公司凭借单一合同实现盈亏平衡。对于Anthropic，分析师采用了CEO Dario Amodei本人公开的估计：AI行业约一半的计算支出用于推理，且推理算力转化为收入时具有3倍的乘数效应。据此，用于推理能力的50亿美元可产生约150亿美元的增量年度经常性收入——对已快速增长的收入基础来说是重大补充。

**超越地球的雄心：轨道AI计算**  
公告中另一个关键方面涉及“轨道AI计算能力”——基本上就是太空中的数据中心。诚然，这听起来像科幻营销语言。但它直接关联到这两家公司以及其他AI巨头日益面临的核心问题：AI基础设施开始超越地球限制。因此，当全球最大的AI公司与建造了全球最大可重复使用火箭系统并运营着数千颗在轨卫星的公司联合发布这样的声明时，我们最好相信太空数据中心可能很快成为现实。

尽管有Mirae Asset的分析，但Colossus交易的实际财务细节并未公开。然而，Anthropic最近在G轮融资中筹集了300亿美元，估值达3800亿美元。不难推测其中部分资金可能用于资助Colossus协议。此外，该公司上个月表示其年化收入运行率已超过300亿美元，凸显了Claude业务当前的惊人规模。

**总结**  
xAI快速建造了Colossus 1——事实证明太快了。由此产生的混合GPU架构造成了结构性的训练低效，使得该集群难以作为长期平台维持。随着Colossus 2现在基于统一的Blackwell硬件正常运行，Colossus 1成为寻找更好用途的第一代资产。需求爆发且算力不足的Anthropic恰好提供了这个机会。这笔交易将实际上正在贬值的负债转化为约60亿美元的年收入——足以使xAI接近盈亏平衡。对Anthropic而言，同样的算力可解锁预估150亿美元的额外年度经常性收入。两家公司各取所需，而马斯克在可能IPO前夕获得了一个引人注目的基础设施故事。
